scope: V03 FWL Leakage Test experiment for kv_verify verification pipeline

findings.description: n_permutations parameter included in run_v03 signature but currently unused. V03 uses AUROC difference thresholds, not permutation null distributions.
findings.impact: No functional impact. Parameter reserved for potential future permutation-based leakage testing.
findings.recommendation: If permutation testing is needed later, implement inside run_v03 rather than adding a separate function.

findings.description: No SHA-256 checksum on result JSON. V04 (the only existing experiment) also omits checksums.
findings.impact: Result integrity not cryptographically verified. Consistent with existing convention.
findings.recommendation: Add checksums to both V03 and V04 if result integrity becomes a concern.

findings.description: Polynomial FWL conditions 3-4 residualize the full dataset before CV splitting, which is itself a form of information leakage. This is by spec design (testing 'does nonlinear length explain the signal?') but means the polynomial AUROC may be optimistically biased toward collapse.
findings.impact: If polynomial FWL appears to collapse signal, it may be partly due to overfitting confounds on the full dataset rather than true nonlinear confounding.
findings.recommendation: Monitor R-squared values for poly3 fits. If R-squared approaches 1.0 for any feature, the polynomial degree is too high for the sample size.

findings.description: Degree-3 polynomial on 2 confounds produces 9 basis features. With N=10-20, this risks overfitting confounds and artificially collapsing all signal.
findings.impact: Poly3 AUROC may be artificially low for small-N comparisons (esp. exp18b with N=10), making FALSIFIED verdict more likely than warranted.
findings.recommendation: Compare poly2 vs poly3 R-squared. If poly3 is dramatically higher, the degree-3 results should be interpreted with extreme caution.

findings.description: Leakage detection uses a fixed 0.05 AUROC threshold without uncertainty quantification. With N=20, AUROC noise is ~0.05-0.10, so threshold may trigger from noise or miss real leakage.
findings.impact: Individual comparison leakage verdicts may have high false positive/negative rates. The overall pattern across 10 comparisons is more informative than any single comparison.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding paired bootstrap CI on AUROC(full) - AUROC(within) for more rigorous leakage detection.

findings.description: Tests run the full experiment (10 comparisons x 4 conditions) for each test method. No caching or fixture sharing between test methods.
findings.impact: Test suite will be slow (~30-60 seconds per test, ~10-20 minutes total). Acceptable for CI but slow for development iteration.
findings.recommendation: Consider a session-scoped pytest fixture that runs run_v03 once and shares the result across all tests. This matches V04's pattern but would improve speed significantly.

referrals.description: Verify polynomial FWL degree 3 does not overfit confounds given small sample sizes. Check R-squared values for degree-3 fits approaching 1.0.
Consider whether a paired bootstrap test on AUROC(full) - AUROC(within) would be more appropriate than a fixed 0.05 threshold for leakage detection.
